title: Seared Halibut with Herb-Caper Sauce
description: Restaurant-quality crust without butter or cream, fast enough for a weeknight. High-heat sear builds flavor that compensates for halibut's low fat content; oven finish keeps it moist.

ingredients.name: halibut fillets, 6–7 oz each (skin-on preferred for structure)

instructions.text: Pat fillets completely dry — this is the single biggest factor in getting a good sear. Let them sit on paper towels for 10 minutes before cooking.
Heat an oven-safe skillet (stainless or cast iron) over medium-high until very hot. Add a thin coat of avocado oil — it handles high heat better than olive oil here.
Sear flesh-side down 2–3 minutes without moving. You want a golden crust. Flip once, then immediately transfer the pan to the oven.
Roast 5–7 minutes depending on thickness. Halibut is done at 130–135°F internal — pull it before it looks fully done, it carries over.
While fish rests, drop heat to medium. Add a splash of olive oil to the same pan, sauté garlic 60 seconds, add capers and lemon juice, stir in parsley (and Dijon if using). Done in 90 seconds.
Spoon sauce over fillets and serve immediately.

notes:
Why this method: halibut is lean, which makes it taste bland when roasted or steamed alone. A high-heat sear builds a crust that adds flavor and texture the fish can't provide on its own. The oven finish cooks it gently and evenly without drying it out.

Good pairings: roasted asparagus or broccolini (425°F, olive oil, salt, 12 minutes); farro or quinoa (batch cook and reheat); simple arugula salad with lemon and walnut oil — walnut oil's flavor is excellent with fish.
